en.extract:
Latham's francolin (Peliperdix lathami) or the forest francolin, is a species of bird in the francolin group of the family Phasianidae. It is the only member of the monotypic genus Peliperdix. It is widespread across the African tropical rainforest.

'''Latham's francolin''' ('''''Peliperdix lathami''''') or the '''forest francolin''', is a species of [[bird]] in the [[francolin]] group of the family [[Phasianidae]]. It is the only member of the [[Monotypic taxon|monotypic genus]] '''''Peliperdix'''''.<ref>{{Cite journal|last=Crowe|first=Timothy M.|last2=Mandiwana-Neudani|first2=Tshifhiwa G.|last3=Donsker|first3=David B.|last4=Bowie|first4=Rauri CK|last5=Little|first5=Robin M.|date=2020-04-02|title=Resolving nomenclatural 'confusion' vis-à-vis Latham's Francolin (Francolinus/Peliperdix/Afrocolinus lathami) and the 'Red-tailed' francolins (Francolinus/Ortygornis/Peliperdix spp.)|journal=Ostrich|volume=91|issue=2|pages=134–136|doi=10.2989/00306525.2020.1723140|issn=0030-6525}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Taxonomic Updates – IOC World Bird List|url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/updates/taxonomy/|access-date=2021-07-14|language=en-US}}</ref> It is widespread across the [[Tropical rainforest|African tropical rainforest]].

[[File:Peliperdix lathami - Royal Museum for Central Africa - DSC06817.JPG|thumb|left|An exhibit at the [[Royal Museum for Central Africa]].]]
